title: 《Nature》子刊实锤：每周只练1次，减脂效果≈练3次！有效燃烧腹部脂肪 | 原文
content: A study in Nature Communications found that weekly high-intensity interval training (HIIT) done just once—known as "weekend warrior" patterns—produces fat-loss and cardiorespiratory benefits comparable to exercising three times per week. Led by a University of Hong Kong team, the research suggests a single weekly HIIT session effectively reduces abdominal fat, offering a time-efficient fitness option for busy individuals.

title: 美国两机构严限学者与外国合著论文，有外国署名就得删 | 原文
content: NIH and NASA are imposing stricter restrictions on U.S. researchers collaborating with foreign institutions. Researchers must obtain pre-approval to publish papers with foreign co-authors, even if all experiments were conducted domestically. NIH has instructed some grantees to remove published papers with foreign collaborators from annual progress reports, while NASA warned that collaborations with Chinese researchers may violate regulations. These requirements were communicated through private emails, causing confusion among scientists.

title: Claude Code Workflow 抓包后原理分析 - Workwith GPT5.5
content: Claude Code's workflow feature uses a JavaScript orchestration script written by the main model and executed locally. The script launches sub-agents via agent() calls, which return structured JSON outputs. These outputs feed into downstream prompts through explicit JS wiring, with pipeline() and parallel() controlling execution flow. Results are injected back into the main session via <task-notification>. In the analyzed example, 21 agents collaborated in a board game design tournament, consuming 232,900 tokens over 258 seconds.

title: [开源] Gold Band--编排 Coding Agent 的桌面端 Harness 应用
content: Gold Band is an open-source desktop application for orchestrating coding AI agents, built with Rust, React, Tauri 2, and Agent Client Protocol (ACP). It addresses challenges in long-running AI coding tasks by adding engineering constraints: workflow orchestration with visual node-based design, role-based context management, AI output validation with DSL expressions, and execution observation. Currently in developer preview, it supports Claude Code via ACP, with planned multi-agent support for Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and others. The tool features configurable retry loops, round-based execution, artifact management, and future plans for parallel execution, dynamic AI routing, and skill/MCP management.

title: 谁是世界上第二爱泡泡玛特的人？
content: Famous investor Duan Yongping has become Pop Mart's second-largest shareholder with a 5.69% stake, worth approximately HK$12.3 billion. This marks a dramatic reversal from his earlier skepticism about the company. After visiting Pop Mart stores in London and reading founder Wang Ning's biography, Duan changed his position, first selling put options to earn premiums, then fully converting his China Shenhua holdings into Pop Mart stock. Pop Mart's 2025 financial results showed explosive growth: revenue of 37.12 billion yuan (up 184.7%) and adjusted net profit of 13.08 billion yuan (up 284.5%). However, concerns persist about whether Pop Mart can sustain its high ROE of 77.52% and replace LABUBU's popularity with other IPs as the company's valuation multiples have compressed significantly.

title: Opus4.8 正式发布，AI 第一次会说「我不确定」了 | 原文
content: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8 on May 28, six weeks after version 4.7. The update focuses on making the AI more reliable rather than delivering dramatic capability jumps. A key improvement is the model's ability to express uncertainty when it lacks confidence, addressing a major pain point where AI previously presented incorrect answers with false certainty.

title: OPPO、荣耀、影石一齐拿出“电子吧唧”
content: OPPO, Honor, and Insta360 recently launched circular e-ink screen accessories marketed as "electronic badges" targeting anime and idol fan culture. OPPO's Bubble and Honor's "Magic Screen" function as magnetic phone second screens for selfies, live images, and social media control, while Insta360's Mic Pro combines a microphone with e-ink display. Priced from $70–$280, these devices tap into China's $2.4 billion "merch economy" by enabling users to display customizable digital images of favorite characters. The trend reflects broader "Cute Tech" movement where consumer electronics shift from pure utility toward fashion accessories emphasizing emotional connection and self-expression, as seen with Tamagotchi's resurgence. However, ecosystem lock-in and high prices may limit adoption beyond enthusiasts.

title: 搜集了 24 个上架跨端 App 的开发者访谈，有些结论和想象中不一样
content: A survey of 24 published cross-platform app developers reveals unexpected framework choices. Nearly half used Apple native tools (Swift/SwiftUI), citing speed to market. Electron powers successful apps like Screen Studio (5,000+ sales), with developers noting poor performance stems from implementation, not the framework itself. Many Tauri adopters switched from Electron due to smaller bundle sizes and mobile support. React Native veterans report painful upgrades and abandoned third-party packages, though Expo has improved. Notably absent: zero cases using Taro, uni-app, or Capacitor, reflecting a gap in Chinese developer participation.

title: [分享] Hermes Memory Sidecar v3.0 — 给任意 AI 智能体加装长期记忆
content: Hermes Memory Sidecar v3.0 adds persistent memory to AI agents without modifying their core code. The sidecar handles session archiving, indexing, governance, and retrieval through a pipeline: session capture, FTS5 and vector index rebuilding, tiered context injection (recent sessions, keyword search, semantic vectors), health monitoring, and maintenance cycles. It supports multiple agents via AGENT_HOME, manages multi-version memory objects with conflict resolution, offers optional vector embeddings, and features "Focused Dossiers" for important entities. Production-proven over six months with 12,600+ sessions.

content: A developer rebuilt their resume as a cyberpunk-themed website using Vite, React, and Remotion. The project uses a monorepo with two source-only packages sharing Zod-validated bilingual data between the website and video components. title: 午休的白领，正在把商场B1变成「钟点房」
content: White-collar workers in China are transforming mall basements into midday rest spaces through "new Chinese-style massage"—quick, affordable 20-40 minute sessions costing under $10. Unlike traditional massage targeting therapeutic results, these stripped-down services offer harried office workers a brief, guilt-free escape: a place to doze, check phones, or simply pause in a "pain-free phone-watching" chair. The trend reflects broader cultural shifts toward compressed leisure, where even rest feels optimized and time-frugal. Though health benefits are minimal, the format succeeds precisely by lowering stakes—cheap, fast, and consequence-light relaxation for workers who feel prolonged rest has become socially unacceptable.

The company also expanded its "intelligent driving safety net" to urban navigation for both new and existing vehicle owners upgrading to Tianyan 5.0. BYD's chip development spans 24 years, involving over 7,000 researchers and 100 billion yuan investment, with more than 2,000 chip products launched. The Tianyan 5.0 system features upgraded architecture, satellite sensor technology, and a planned physics AI model, while addressing cost, usability, and safety concerns for autonomous driving adoption.

title: 裁掉的人，养不起的 Token
content: Silicon Valley companies face a paradox with AI spending: Uber exhausted its annual AI budget in four months with 70% of code submissions AI-generated, yet cannot link token costs to user value. Meanwhile, 80% of large enterprises deploying AI have cut jobs, but Gartner found zero correlation between layoffs and ROI. AI-driven layoffs serve as signals to investors rather than creating business value. Companies struggle with unpredictable token-based pricing versus traditional software subscriptions. Ultimately, money flows from workers and unpredictable budgets directly to AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI, reshaping corporate spending without proven returns.

title: 刚刚，Claude Opus 4.8来了，两个史上首次改写历史
content: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8, reclaiming top performance in global AI benchmarks. The model scores 0% on both "lie rate" and "laziness rate"—industry firsts—meaning it never reports false data or skips investigation tasks. It achieves 69.2% on SWE-Bench Pro, 83% win rate on FrontierSWE, and reduces token usage by 35% versus its predecessor. New features include adjustable effort control (Low to Max), discounted fast mode, and "dynamic workflows" enabling hundreds of parallel subagents, which recently rewrote 750,000 lines of code in 11 days. Anthropic also closed a $65 billion funding round at a $965 billion valuation, surpassing OpenAI, and teased upcoming Claude Mythos.

title: 刚刚，Claude Opus 4.8 上线，张口就说自己是 DeepSeek、Qwen
content: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.8, upgrading its flagship model with improved coding, agent tasks, reasoning, and knowledge work while maintaining current pricing. The release was accompanied by a $65 billion Series H funding round at a $96.5 billion valuation. Key new features include effort control for adjustable reasoning intensity and dynamic workflows enabling multi-agent collaboration for complex engineering tasks. Users reported the model occasionally misidentifying itself as DeepSeek or Qwen. Anthropic is expanding infrastructure through partnerships with Amazon, Google, Broadcom, and SpaceX to support growing compute demands.

content: Apple is planning its largest MacBook Pro redesign, featuring an OLED touchscreen with macOS optimization, a hole-punch camera with Dynamic Island replacing the notch, and a 2nm M6 processor. The new model may be named "MacBook Ultra," positioned above current M5 Pro/Max variants, with an expected release in October or November 2026.

title: 消息称国家大基金领投 DeepSeek，投前估值 450 亿美元
content: DeepSeek, a Chinese AI large language model company, is nearing completion of its first external funding round since its founding three years ago. The China Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund is leading the round, which values the company at $45 billion pre-money and aims to raise approximately $10 billion. If completed, this would represent the largest Series A fundraising in China's AI large model history. Multiple market-oriented investment institutions are also in negotiations, with the investor list expected to be finalized by month-end.

title: 字节跳动据悉正开发定制 CPU 以支持 AI 需求
content: ByteDance is developing custom CPUs to support its AI infrastructure needs, addressing chip price surges and supply constraints. The move reflects the industry's shift toward the "inference" phase, which demands significantly more CPU power. The company plans to deploy these processors in its own servers and data centers to support internal operations and prepare for launching agent-based products like its Coze platform. ByteDance is simultaneously pursuing both Arm-based and open-source RISC-V architectures to determine which better suits long-term data center requirements. The initiative accelerates following substantial price increases from previous suppliers Intel and AMD, which raised prices by 10-35% quarter-over-quarter.

title: 国产内存龙头长鑫科技 IPO 过会
content: Chinese memory chip leader Changxin Technology's Shanghai STAR Market IPO was approved, targeting a record 29.5 billion yuan. Its Hefei facility is active with semiconductor firms clustering nearby. Amid a memory super-cycle, standard DDR products now yield higher margins than advanced HBM chips, prompting Micron to restart DDR4 production. As memory becomes critical AI infrastructure, foreign institutions are applying growth stock valuations to global memory giants, potentially reshaping industry valuation frameworks.

title: 谷歌工程师因涉嫌在 Polymarket 预测市场进行内幕交易遭起诉
content: Google software engineer Michele Spagnuolo was charged with insider trading on prediction market Polymarket. He allegedly used internal access to 2025 Google search data to bet that singer D4vd would be the most-searched person, earning over $1.2 million. Google has suspended him and is cooperating with law enforcement, stating the act severely violates company policy.

title: 马斯克：SpaceX 与 Anthropic 达成的 Colossus AI 租赁协议仅为期六个月
content: SpaceX CEO Musk stated the Colossus AI training data center cluster lease to Anthropic is initially six months, though potentially extendable. Earlier reports indicated Anthropic would pay $1.25 billion monthly for computing access through May 2029. Musk clarified it is a 180-day rental with 90-day termination notice by either party, noting SpaceX may reclaim resources if computing availability becomes constrained.

title: Meta 将推出 AI 聊天机器人订阅服务 月费最低 7.99 美元
content: Meta will launch an AI chatbot subscription service with two tiers: Meta One Plus at $7.99 monthly for users frequently generating images, video, and using complex reasoning; and Meta One Premium at $19.99 for identical features with higher usage limits. The move aims to offset substantial AI investments.

title: 外媒：黄仁勋加入清华大学，任职经管学院顾问
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has joined the advisory committee of Tsinghua University's School of Economics and Management. The committee, chaired by Apple CEO Tim Cook, connects global business leaders with Chinese academia. Huang's appointment follows his May visit to China and allows him to maintain ties with Chinese academic, tech, and business circles while complying with U.S. export controls. Meanwhile, Huang received an honorary doctorate from Carnegie Mellon University, where Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan confirmed Intel and Nvidia are collaborating on new products.

title: 阿里达摩院首次发布 GPU 版本求解器 突破亿级变量「不可解」难题
content: Alibaba's DAMO Academy released a GPU-based version of its MindOpt solver, leveraging parallel acceleration to solve previously intractable optimization problems. Testing on approximately 2,000 benchmark cases showed stable high-precision solutions for over 99% of problem types, including billion-variable linear programming problems that were traditionally unsolvable. The solver addresses computational challenges in power grid scheduling, flight planning, advanced manufacturing, and financial management—domains where conventional CPU-based solvers suffered from exponential memory demands and limited parallelism, causing hours-long convergence failures or system crashes.

title: Training Azerbaijani language models on Amazon SageMaker AI
content: Azercell Telecom collaborated with AWS to build an Azerbaijani language model on Amazon SageMaker AI, addressing challenges of limited training data for this morphologically rich language. The three-stage solution developed a custom tokenizer that doubled encoding efficiency (from 3.22 to 1.59 tokens per word), continued pre-training of Llama 3.2 1B with FSDP and Liger Kernel optimizations achieving 23% higher throughput and 58% lower GPU memory, and LoRA fine-tuning for conversational ability. The framework runs as ephemeral SageMaker training jobs, eliminating idle costs, with configurations validated across GPU instances for scalable deployment.

title: 如何看待5月29日发布的Claude4.8Opus疑似大量蒸馏国内开源模型？
content: On May 29, Anthropic released Claude 4.8 Opus, which showed significant benchmark improvements. However, Chinese AI communities quickly observed that Opus 4.8's outputs under certain prompts resembled domestic Chinese open-source models. This raised questions given Anthropic's previous strong criticism of model distillation practices. A similar incident occurred in 2022 when Claude 2.1 identified itself as Baidu's Ernie Bot.

content: In NBA Western Conference Finals Game 6, the San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 118-91, tying the series 3-3. The Spurs established early control through strong three-point shooting from Champagnie and Wembanyama, maintained a 7-point halftime lead, then broke the game open with a 20-0 run in the third quarter to build a nearly 30-point advantage. The Thunder's scoring drought proved decisive as the Spurs forced a decisive Game 7.

title: 比亚迪智能驾驶为什么能兜底？
content: BYD can offer accident coverage for its intelligent driving system because it fully owns the entire technology chain. Unlike competitors that rely on third-party suppliers for algorithms, sensors, and braking systems—creating accountability gaps when accidents occur—BYD develops its own autonomous driving software, manufactures its own chassis components through subsidiary Fudi, controls all vehicle data internally, and operates its own insurance company (BYD Property & Casualty). This vertical integration eliminates blame-shifting, enables precise risk modeling using complete user data, and allows BYD to absorb costs controllably. The company also leverages coverage claims to collect rare accident data, improving its systems and widening its competitive lead.
